zh:
阿坎人的神圣世界横跨今日加纳与科特迪瓦南部，在阿散蒂、芳蒂、阿夸皮姆等社群中各有口音与祭法。至高者尼亚梅居于天上，大地以阿萨瑟·亚之名承受耕种、埋葬与誓言；河流、岩石与森林里的「阿博松」不是抽象象征，而是有脾气、有禁日、会降临祭司身体的力量。祖先、家族魂与个人灵性又织在其中。英国殖民官R·S·拉特雷留下了二十世纪初最庞大的阿散蒂记录，但他的译名与分类同样带着时代局限；这里以活态信仰的多种说法校正那副旧镜片。

阿坎宇宙最响亮的讲述者却是一只蜘蛛。阿南西向尼亚梅买下世间故事，用机巧捕获豹、蜂与精灵；他贪婪、怯懦、聪明得危险，常在自己的计谋里摔得四脚朝天。与他相对的是森林深处的萨萨邦萨姆与姆莫阿蒂亚：前者把铁齿和长腿藏进树冠，后者是脚跟向前的小灵，会传授草药，也会把冒犯者引入密林。阿库阿芭木偶则保存另一种奇迹——不孕的阿库阿背着木女儿承受嘲笑，终于生下孩子，于是雕像成了千万次祈愿的形状。从天神到河灵，从蜘蛛故事到母亲背后的木像，这套传统把神圣安放在社会秩序与一切需要兑现的关系里。
en:
The sacred world of the Akan spans southern Ghana and Côte d’Ivoire and speaks differently among Asante, Fante, Akuapem, and other communities. Nyame dwells above; earth bears cultivation, burial, and oath under the name Asase Yaa. The abosom of rivers, rocks, and forests are not literary symbols but powers with temperaments, forbidden days, and priests through whom they may descend. Ancestors, lineage souls, and personal spirit are woven through the same field. The British colonial officer R. S. Rattray produced the largest early twentieth-century record of Asante religion, but his translations and categories bear the limits of their era; the atlas reads that archive beside later Akan scholarship and living practice.

Its most famous narrator is a spider. Ananse purchases the world’s stories from Nyame by trapping leopard, hornet, and spirit through dangerous cleverness. He is greedy, fearful, and brilliant enough to fall into his own designs. Deeper in the forest wait Sasabonsam and the mmoatia: the first hangs iron teeth and impossible legs among the branches; the second are backward-footed beings who may teach medicine or turn an offender from the road. The akua’ba figure preserves another wonder. A woman named Akua carries a wooden daughter through public mockery and at last bears a child, making the image a vessel for countless prayers. From sky and river to spider tale and wooden child, Akan tradition locates sacred force within social order and every relationship that must be honored.
